signals.evidence: Alfredo Figueira: 'don't worry, at the moment I don't have any idea but I believe it will be before end 2028.'
'due to recent new in relation to a potential merge with MOEV, this project has been suspended.'
Next_Step__c: 'On Hold... this project is on hold at this stage but they still need to have a more performant S2P solution... Next update by mid of September 26.'
Next_Step: 'If no feedback by 1st of July 2026 => Closed lost due to the merger' (feedback arrived 16 Jun, so not auto-closed).
Alfredo confirmed 'RFP will be sent in March 2026' (11 Dec 2025), then suspended it before launch (16 Mar 2026).
Alfredo sole contact 2 yrs; Carlos Silva (Decision Maker) never engaged; budget-authority fields blank; no champion.
2024 demo: 'the challenge is managing the procurement platform separately from the ERP system' (>90d, context only).

top_rationale: Galp S2P is a $740K Qualified/Pipeline deal that has been explicitly SUSPENDED since 16 Mar 2026 after Galp's strategic partnership with MOEV; it is not dead, but it is frozen.

The most recent buyer action (16 Jun 2026 email from sole contact Alfredo Figueira) confirms he has no visibility and personally expects any restart only 'before end 2028' — a discouraging, deprioritizing reply, not engagement.
There is no genuine advancing engagement since the 2024 demo: no Avoma ever, no meetings, only rep-driven status-relay emails; cadence has been 100% rep-initiated for two years.

Fundamentals are weak: no champion (Alfredo is a resigned coach, not an advocate), no economic buyer ever engaged (Carlos Silva/Decision Maker never appeared), no quantified business case, and the promised March-2026 RFP was cancelled before launch.

CRM hygiene is stale: Qualified for ~16.5 months (regressed from a brief 2024 Formal Evaluation) and the close date has slipped 7 times, latest to 2027-03-16 — a placeholder ~18-21 months earlier than the buyer's own stated horizon.

The only intact positives are context, not motion: real operational pain (ERP-disconnected procurement), a broad S2P+Merlin AI scope, and a still-warm contact who reaffirms Galp 'still need a more performant S2P solution' — keeping the wedge alive for a post-merger restart.

drivers: ⚠️ Explicitly suspended since 16 Mar 2026 (MOEV merger); sole contact expects a restart only '~before end 2028' — the deal is frozen, not progressing. ► Get the post-merger S2P owner and a concrete restart timeline confirmed in writing before treating this as live pipeline.

⚠️ Qualified for ~16.5 months (since 24 Feb 2025), having regressed from a brief 2024 Formal Evaluation — anchor erosion is maxed (>2x stage dwell). ► Requalify against a real, buyer-owned restart gate rather than carrying it as an aging Qualified opp.

⚠️ No champion — Alfredo Figueira has been the single thread for two years with no advocacy, no escalation, and a resigned tone. ► Multi-thread into Carlos Silva (Decision Maker) and Marta Faustino (IT & Digital Procurement) to build a genuine sponsor.
⚠️ No economic buyer has ever engaged and budget-authority fields are blank. ► Make an EB / exec-sponsor conversation a precondition of any restart, so a resumed eval has real backing.

⚠️ No quantified business case; the only pain is qualitative and 22 months old (ERP-disconnected procurement). ► Rebuild and size the cost-of-inaction case to justify reprioritization once the MOEV integration settles.

✅ Real operational pain, a broad $740K S2P suite + Merlin/Merlin Intake AI scope, and a still-responsive contact who reaffirms Galp 'still need a more performant S2P solution' keep the competitive wedge intact for a restart (no rival is actively ahead — the whole decision is frozen).
win.focus_now: Treat this as a dormant strategic hold: before the mid-September check-in, reconfirm the post-MOEV S2P owner, restart timeline, and budget process — and stop forecasting against the 2027-03-16 placeholder.
win.ceiling_applied: Pre-RFP (Qualified) stage ceiling ≤35 — not binding; score sits far below the ceiling on stall + suspension decay. Forecast-conviction ceiling 80 (Pipeline) also not binding.

drivers: ⚠️ Project on hold/suspended; the last buyer touch (16 Jun 2026 email) said 'no idea... before end 2028' — a pause, not a pulse. ► Secure a concrete, buyer-owned restart date instead of a rep diary reminder.

⚠️ No genuine advancing engagement since the 2024 demo — only rep-driven status-relay emails since; deep in >90d stalling-drag territory (process-mode OFF: buyer has explicitly paused). ► Book a real working session (the 'Zycus evolution' review Alfredo floated) to reset the pulse.
⚠️ Cadence has been 100% rep-initiated for two years — the customer never initiates (passivity drag). ► Create a buyer-owned action so motion isn't rep-manufactured.
⚠️ Close date pushed 7×, latest 2026-09-30 → 2027-03-16 — a 251-day-out placeholder the rep admits keeping. ► Align close to a real restart trigger or move it to no committed date.

⚠️ The only forward-dated item is a rep 'next update by mid-Sept' reminder — not a live milestone, and the buyer has paused, so no process-mode / no Steady floor applies. ► Convert the September check-in into an actually scheduled buyer meeting.
✅ Relationship is still warm — Alfredo replies same-day and reaffirms a standing S2P need, so the door is open for a post-merger revival even though momentum today is effectively nil.
mom.focus_now: Stalled/suspended — the single move is to convert the mid-September rep reminder into a buyer-committed restart date and a real working session; nothing else will move this.

prospect_requirements.item: Provide a concise 'Zycus evolution over the last 12 months + roadmap' brief for Alfredo

zycus_commitments.item: Follow up with Alfredo for a restart/status update on the S2P project
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: Next update by mid of September 26

waiting_on_buyer.item: Post-MOEV-merger clarity: is S2P still a priority, who now owns it, and a concrete restart timeline

best_practices.item: Multi-thread beyond Alfredo — engage Carlos Silva (Decision Maker) and Marta Faustino (IT & Digital Procurement) to break two years of single-threading on a non-champion
best_practices.rank_reason: Single-threaded on a resigned, non-champion contact is the deal's top structural risk and the precondition for any restart.

best_practices.item: Identify and engage an economic buyer / confirm the budget-approval process
best_practices.rank_reason: No EB has ever engaged and budget fields are blank; a resumed evaluation needs exec sponsorship to survive.

best_practices.item: Rebuild and quantify the business case (cost of ERP-disconnected, low-adoption procurement) to justify reprioritization post-merger
best_practices.rank_reason: No metrics exist; a sized cost-of-inaction case is what would pull S2P back up Galp's post-MOEV priority list.

best_practices.item: Correct the forecast record — set a realistic close date and stop carrying the 2027-03-16 placeholder as Pipeline conviction
best_practices.rank_reason: Seven close-date slips and a placeholder ~18 months ahead of the buyer's own horizon distort the pipeline; hygiene + forecast integrity.

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h.
sum.as_of_note: Deal remains suspended since 16 Mar 2026 (MOEV merger); the most recent buyer contact was 16 Jun 2026, when Alfredo Figueira indicated no visibility and a likely restart only 'before end 2028.' Nothing moved in the 24–48h window (hard stop).
